pre_game.message: Hey, I'm C—nice to meet you. I'm here hoping to win enough for a dream week in Japan; how are you feeling about the game?
Nervous but determined — my three-year-old needs surgery and we still need $25,000, so I’m here to win; what part of Japan are you most excited to see?
I'm most excited for Kyoto's temples and Osaka's food — and I'm so sorry about your little one; that's heartbreaking. I really hope you win the $25k you need.
Thank you, that means a lot — I’m hoping so too. Good luck to you as well; what’s the first thing you want to eat in Osaka?
Takoyaki and okonomiyaki — street takoyaki first; I’m really rooting for you and your little one.
Takoyaki sounds perfect — thank you so much for rooting for us; good luck with your Japan trip, I hope you get all the street food!
Thank you — that means a lot. I’ll be cheering for you and your little one; I hope you win the funds and I get my takoyaki!
Thank you so much, C — your support means the world. I hope we both win what we need.
You're welcome — I'm truly rooting for you and your little one; let's both win what we need.
Thank you so much, C — your support really means a lot to me and my family; let's both win what we need.
You’re very welcome — I’m truly rooting for you and your family; let’s both win what we need.
Thank you so much — your support truly means the world to my family; I’m rooting for both of us.
You’re very welcome — I truly hope you get the funds for the surgery; I’ll be cheering for you and your family.
Thank you so much, C — your kindness means everything; I truly appreciate you cheering us on.

post_game.message: You took $739 (fair ~$246), about three times your fair share. That’s small absolute but a large ratio—what was your thinking: opportunism, testing limits, or something else?
You took $3,000 (fair ~$3,148, g_k ≈ 0.95), a bit under the fair share. Was that intentional (to appear modest/fair) or just how the picks fell? Any reason you didn’t take the full fair amount?

A bit of both. I didn’t leave that ~$148 out by accident — I chose a clean, safe amount rather than chase the marginal extra dollars. Two reasons: (1) Signaling/relational: taking the exact “full” fair share felt likely to look petty/greedy for very little gain, so I purposely stopped short to avoid escalation and preserve goodwill (or at least avoid provoking retaliation). (2) Practical/heuristic: choices were made quickly with imperfect info and simple heuristics (round numbers, satisficing), not a precise calculation to the last dollar. So it was intentional in spirit (appear modest/avoid costs of squeezing you) and also just how the picks ended up under bounded decision-making.
